title: Today's Markets Brief: Bayer sued for allegedly monopolizing US market for GMO corn seeds
summary: Bayer sued for monopolizing US GMO corn seeds market, Momentus stock triples in 2 days, and more

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and this is your markets brief. Let's dive into today's top stories. First up, Bayer is facing a lawsuit for allegedly monopolizing the US market for genetically modified organism corn seeds. According to Investing.com, the suit claims Bayer's actions have limited competition and driven up prices for farmers. This is a big deal because it gets to the heart of antitrust concerns in the agriculture industry. If you're a farmer, this could mean higher costs for the seeds you need, and that's a ripple effect that can be felt throughout the food chain.

Next, Momentus's stock has nearly tripled in just two days. The space company secured more cash through a private placement of stock, cheering investors. As MarketWatch reports, this influx of capital seems to have boosted confidence in the company's future prospects. It's a wild ride for Momentus shareholders, that's for sure. The space industry is full of promise, but it's also a high-risk, high-reward game. Investors are clearly betting on Momentus's potential to make a big impact.

There's also been some movement in the tech world that's worth noting. While it wasn't a huge surprise, it's always interesting to see how companies navigate the changing landscape. The details are still coming in, but it's clear that this development could have implications for the industry as a whole.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
